subject: [PATCH v6 3/3] x86/tdx: Fix zero-extension for 32-bit port I/O

reviews.author: tip-bot2 for Kiryl Shutsemau (Meta)
reviews.summary: The patch was successfully merged into the x86/tdx branch of tip, indicating a positive sentiment from the reviewer.

2026-07-13.patch_summary: This patch series addresses a bug in the x86/tdx port I/O handling, specifically for 32-bit operations. The current implementation only masks the lower 32 bits of RAX, preserving the upper 32 bits when emulating a 32-bit port IN instruction. The fix uses insn_assign_reg() to write the result back into RAX with proper partial-register-write semantics.
